scenarios.title: Rogue DHCP Servers / DHCP Leakage
scenarios.problem: Clients receive wrong IP ranges or inconsistent leases due to rogue DHCP and VLAN leakage.
symptoms: DHCP offers appear on customer VLANs
fix_commands: /ip firewall filter add chain=forward protocol=udp dst-port=67-68 action=drop comment="Block rogue DHCP"
/ip firewall filter add chain=forward src-address-list=!trusted_dhcp_servers protocol=udp dst-port=67-68 action=drop
/interface bridge vlan add bridge=BR_PPPoE tagged=bridge,etherX vlan-ids=20

scenarios.title: DHCP Flapping / Reassigning IPs
scenarios.problem: Clients continuously renew or appear to re-identify due to timing and L2 instability.
symptoms: Same client appears with different MACs

scenarios.title: Splynx Blocking / Access Issues
scenarios.problem: Users are blocked or lose access due to Splynx address list or firewall chain issues.

scenarios.title: PPPoE Discovery Issues / Session Failures
scenarios.problem: Clients fail to establish PPPoE sessions due to binding, MTU, or VLAN problems.

scenarios.title: VLAN Misconfiguration / Traffic Bleeding
scenarios.problem: Traffic crosses between mgmt, customer, and security networks due to bad VLAN definitions.
symptoms: Management network reachable from customer side
fix_commands: /interface bridge vlan add bridge=BR_PPPoE vlan-ids=10 tagged=bridge,ether1
/interface bridge vlan add bridge=BR_PPPoE vlan-ids=20 tagged=bridge,ether1
/interface bridge vlan add bridge=BR_PPPoE vlan-ids=30 tagged=bridge,ether1

scenarios.title: Loop / Broadcast Storm Issues
scenarios.problem: Network instability caused by L2 loops and excessive flooding.

scenarios.title: Wireless Backhaul / Bridge Path Issues
scenarios.problem: Traffic fails across wireless backhaul because the path is treated as L2 when it is actually L3.

scenarios.title: MAC Address Inconsistencies
scenarios.problem: Same client appears under multiple MAC identities due to retry behavior and path instability.
scenarios.notes: Often not a rogue device. Can be caused by DHCP/PPPoE retry behavior and unstable packet timing.

scenarios.title: LTE Data Usage / ZeroTier Overhead
scenarios.problem: Unexpected cellular usage caused by overlay management traffic.
symptoms: LTE consumption high while mostly idle

scenarios.title: Hardware Offloading Issues
scenarios.problem: CRS or bridge switching falls back to CPU instead of hardware forwarding.
fix_commands: Confirm the bridge design matches the switch-chip offload model and look for the H flag on /interface bridge port print
Remove offload blockers such as unsupported bridge features, then re-check the affected ports instead of trying to set a generic hw=yes knob

scenarios.title: Option 82 / DHCP Architecture Testing
scenarios.problem: Migration from PPPoE to DHCP with relay and Option 82 insertion.
symptoms: Need port/site identity in DHCP requests

scenarios.title: Firewall / HTTPS Blocking
scenarios.problem: Need to block HTTPS destinations like YouTube using TLS host matching.
fix_commands: /ip firewall filter add chain=forward tls-host=*.youtube.com action=drop

scenarios.title: General Debug Toolkit
scenarios.problem: Core diagnostic command set for MikroTik troubleshooting.
symptoms: Need quick visibility into live state

scenarios.title: Interface Flapping / Link Instability
scenarios.problem: Link repeatedly drops and returns due to negotiation problems.
fix_commands: /interface ethernet set ether1 auto-negotiation=no speed=1Gbps full-duplex=yes

scenarios.title: Bridge Misbinding
scenarios.problem: Expected traffic does not pass because an interface is not actually in the bridge.

scenarios.title: ARP Table Overflow / Instability
scenarios.problem: Neighbor table limits contribute to instability in larger L2 domains.

scenarios.title: MTU Blackhole
scenarios.problem: Large packets are silently dropped, breaking some traffic but not all.

scenarios.title: FastTrack Breaking QoS / Visibility
scenarios.problem: FastTrack bypasses queues and hides traffic from normal inspection paths.

scenarios.title: Queue Tree Not Applying
scenarios.problem: Queue tree configuration exists but does not classify traffic as intended.

scenarios.title: SFP Module Not Recognized
scenarios.problem: Fiber or copper SFP link does not come up as expected.

scenarios.title: NAT Not Working
scenarios.problem: Clients have private connectivity but cannot reach the internet due to missing or broken srcnat.
symptoms: Clients get IP but no internet
fix_commands: /ip firewall nat add chain=srcnat out-interface=ether1 action=masquerade

scenarios.title: Connection Tracking Saturation
scenarios.problem: Conntrack table fills and causes performance or session problems.

scenarios.title: Incorrect Default Route
scenarios.problem: No outbound connectivity because default route is missing or wrong.
symptoms: Ping to local gateway works but beyond does not

scenarios.title: Asymmetric Routing
scenarios.problem: Traffic exits one way and returns another, breaking stateful flows.
fix_commands: /ip route rule add src-address=SUBNET action=lookup table=main

scenarios.title: DNS Not Resolving
scenarios.problem: Internet reachable by IP but not by domain due to DNS configuration issues.

scenarios.title: CPU Spikes from Broadcast Storm
scenarios.problem: Broadcast flooding drives CPU and degrades switching performance.

scenarios.title: Management Access Exposed
scenarios.problem: Administrative services are reachable from untrusted networks.
fix_commands: /ip firewall filter add chain=input in-interface=ether1 action=drop

scenarios.title: Packet Fragmentation Issues
scenarios.problem: Large TCP sessions break due to MTU/MSS mismatch.
fix_commands: /ip firewall mangle add chain=forward action=change-mss new-mss=clamp-to-pmtu protocol=tcp tcp-flags=syn

scenarios.title: Duplicate IP Addresses
scenarios.problem: Two devices compete for the same address, causing ARP instability.

scenarios.title: Route Flapping
scenarios.problem: Dynamic routes repeatedly appear and disappear, causing path instability.
fix_commands: /routing ospf interface-template set [find where interfaces=ether1] cost=10

scenarios.title: STP Blocking Wrong Port
scenarios.problem: Bridge protocol selects the wrong forwarding path.

scenarios.title: Wireless Interface Not Passing Traffic
scenarios.problem: Client or AP associates, but data does not bridge correctly.
fix_commands: Verify the active wireless package and datapath/bridge binding for that interface before changing anything; do not assume legacy bridge-mode applies on every RouterOS 7.18+ wireless stack

scenarios.title: Firewall Dropping Legit Traffic
scenarios.problem: Rule order or broad match conditions block valid flows.

scenarios.title: Bridge Horizon Misuse
scenarios.problem: Ports are isolated from each other due to inappropriate bridge horizon settings.
symptoms: Ports cannot reach each other when they should

scenarios.title: Invalid RADIUS Authentication
scenarios.problem: RADIUS settings prevent PPPoE authentication from succeeding.

scenarios.title: VLAN Not Tagged on Uplink
scenarios.problem: Expected VLAN does not traverse the uplink because it is missing from the bridge VLAN table.
symptoms: No upstream connectivity for one VLAN
fix_commands: /interface bridge vlan add bridge=BR_PPPoE tagged=ether1 vlan-ids=20

scenarios.title: DHCP Relay Not Working
scenarios.problem: Relay exists but does not pass enough metadata or requests do not reach server correctly.

scenarios.title: Interface Queue Drops
scenarios.problem: Software queues or poor queue configuration cause packet loss under load.

scenarios.title: Power Issues on PoE Port
scenarios.problem: Connected device does not boot due to PoE delivery settings.

scenarios.title: Neighbor Discovery Noise
scenarios.problem: Discovery protocols create unnecessary traffic or exposure on inappropriate interfaces.

log_parsing_module.purpose: Teach the model to parse MikroTik live and historical logs using /log print where ... patterns.

core_patterns.goal: Show only recent warnings and errors
Show entries from a given interface name

reasoning_rules: Start broad, then narrow. Example: first /log print where message~"dhcp", then search for a client MAC, interface name, or IP.
Correlate log lines with sniffer, torch, route, ARP, and bridge host output rather than trusting logs alone.
Treat repeated deassign/reassign, link up/down, and authentication retries as timing or L2 instability signals.
For subscriber issues, search logs by PPPoE username, RADIUS, DHCP lease IP, MAC, interface, and VLAN name.
Look for sequence patterns: interface flap -> DHCP retry -> alternate MAC or auth failure.

example_queries.input: Customer keeps losing IP every few minutes
PPPoE session will not come up
